openshift.title: OpenShift MCP Server
openshift.description: OpenShift MCP server for cluster operations: namespaces and projects, workloads (pods, resources), Helm, monitoring (Prometheus, Alertmanager), OpenShift Service Mesh, kcp workspaces, and OpenShift Virtualization (KubeVirt) VM create and lifecycle. Uses the active kubeconfig context with elevated permissions.

tools.name: events_list
tools.description: List Kubernetes events from all or specific namespaces

tools.name: namespaces_list
tools.description: List all Kubernetes namespaces in the cluster

tools.name: projects_list
tools.description: List all OpenShift projects in the cluster

tools.name: nodes_log
tools.description: Get logs from Kubernetes nodes (kubelet, kube-proxy, system logs)

tools.name: nodes_stats_summary
tools.description: Get detailed resource usage statistics from nodes

tools.name: nodes_top
tools.description: List CPU and memory consumption for nodes

tools.name: pods_list
tools.description: List all pods in the cluster from all namespaces

tools.name: pods_list_in_namespace
tools.description: List pods in a specific namespace

tools.name: pods_get
tools.description: Get a specific pod by name and namespace

tools.name: pods_delete
tools.description: Delete a pod by name and namespace

tools.name: pods_top
tools.description: List resource consumption for pods

tools.name: pods_exec
tools.description: Execute commands in pod containers

tools.name: pods_log
tools.description: Get logs from pod containers

tools.name: pods_run
tools.description: Run a new pod with specified container image

tools.name: resources_list
tools.description: List Kubernetes resources by apiVersion and kind

tools.name: resources_get
tools.description: Get specific Kubernetes resources

tools.name: resources_create_or_update
tools.description: Create or update resources from YAML/JSON

tools.name: resources_delete
tools.description: Delete Kubernetes resources

tools.name: resources_scale
tools.description: Get or update resource scale (replicas)

tools.name: configuration_contexts_list
tools.description: List available kubeconfig contexts and server URLs

tools.name: targets_list
tools.description: List all available targets

tools.name: configuration_view
tools.description: Get current Kubernetes configuration as YAML

tools.name: helm_install
tools.description: Install Helm charts with optional values

tools.name: helm_list
tools.description: List Helm releases in namespaces

tools.name: helm_uninstall
tools.description: Uninstall Helm releases

tools.name: prometheus_query
tools.description: Execute instant PromQL queries for current metrics

tools.name: prometheus_query_range
tools.description: Execute range PromQL queries for time-series data

tools.name: alertmanager_alerts
tools.description: Query active and pending alerts from Alertmanager

tools.name: vm_clone
tools.description: Clone a KubeVirt VirtualMachine via a VirtualMachineClone resource (source name/namespace, target name).

tools.name: vm_create
tools.description: Create virtual machines with instance types and preferences (KubeVirt)

tools.name: vm_lifecycle
tools.description: Manage VM lifecycle - start, stop, restart (KubeVirt)

tools.name: kcp_workspaces_list
tools.description: List available kcp workspaces

tools.name: kcp_workspace_describe
tools.description: Get detailed workspace information

tools.name: ossm_mesh_graph
tools.description: Get mesh topology and health overview (OpenShift Service Mesh)

tools.name: ossm_manage_istio_config_read
tools.description: List or get Istio configuration objects

tools.name: ossm_manage_istio_config
tools.description: Create, patch, or delete Istio configurations

tools.name: ossm_get_resource_details
tools.description: Get details for mesh services and workloads

tools.name: ossm_get_metrics
tools.description: Get traffic metrics for mesh resources

tools.name: ossm_workload_logs
tools.description: Get logs for workload pods

tools.name: ossm_get_traces
tools.description: Get distributed traces for mesh resources

openshift-self-managed.title: Assisted Installer MCP (self-managed)
openshift-self-managed.description: Red Hat Hybrid Cloud Assisted Installer API MCP for self-managed clusters (OCP, SNO): create clusters, ISO and credentials, nmstate networking, hosts, versions, operators, events, and logs. Requires OFFLINE_TOKEN and uses INVENTORY_URL for the assisted-install v2 API.

tools.name: cluster_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a cluster by ID (status, platform, networking, hosts).

tools.name: list_clusters
tools.description: List all Assisted Installer clusters for the account (minimal info per cluster).

tools.name: create_cluster
tools.description: Create a new cluster and infraenv (name, version, base_domain, single_node, ssh key, etc.).

tools.name: set_cluster_vips
tools.description: Set API and ingress VIPs for the cluster.

tools.name: set_cluster_platform
tools.description: Set or update cluster platform configuration for the chosen provider.

tools.name: install_cluster
tools.description: Trigger installation for a cluster that is ready to install.

tools.name: set_cluster_ssh_key
tools.description: Set or update the SSH public key for cluster node access.

tools.name: analyze_cluster_logs
tools.description: Analyze cluster logs for troubleshooting (registered only when ENABLE_TROUBLESHOOTING_TOOLS is set).

tools.name: cluster_events
tools.description: Get events for a cluster by ID.

tools.name: host_events
tools.description: Get events for a specific host in a cluster.

tools.name: cluster_iso_download_url
tools.description: Get discovery ISO download URL(s) for a cluster.

tools.name: cluster_credentials_download_url
tools.description: Get kubeconfig / credentials download URL after installation.

tools.name: cluster_logs_download_url
tools.description: Get a URL to download diagnostic log bundles.

tools.name: list_versions
tools.description: List OpenShift versions available for installation.

tools.name: list_operator_bundles
tools.description: List optional operator bundles addable at install time.

tools.name: add_operator_bundle_to_cluster
tools.description: Request an operator bundle to be installed with the cluster.

tools.name: set_host_role
tools.description: Set host role (auto-assign, master, arbiter, worker) for a discovered host.

tools.name: validate_nmstate_yaml
tools.description: Validate nmstate YAML before applying to hosts.

tools.name: generate_nmstate_yaml
tools.description: Generate initial nmstate YAML from gathered inputs (prefer calling this before hand-authoring YAML).

tools.name: alter_static_network_config_nmstate_for_host
tools.description: Update per-host static network nmstate configuration.

tools.name: list_static_network_config
tools.description: List static network configuration for the cluster or hosts.

openshift-ocm-managed.title: OCM MCP (managed clusters)
openshift-ocm-managed.description: Same assisted-service-mcp binary and tool surface as self-managed; container sets INVENTORY_URL to the OCM clusters_mgmt API for ROSA, ARO, and OSD. Requires OFFLINE_TOKEN.

tools.name: cluster_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a cluster by ID (status, platform, networking, hosts).

tools.name: list_clusters
tools.description: List all Assisted Installer clusters for the account (minimal info per cluster).

tools.name: create_cluster
tools.description: Create a new cluster and infraenv (name, version, base_domain, single_node, ssh key, etc.).

tools.name: set_cluster_vips
tools.description: Set API and ingress VIPs for the cluster.

tools.name: set_cluster_platform
tools.description: Set or update cluster platform configuration for the chosen provider.

tools.name: install_cluster
tools.description: Trigger installation for a cluster that is ready to install.

tools.name: set_cluster_ssh_key
tools.description: Set or update the SSH public key for cluster node access.

tools.name: analyze_cluster_logs
tools.description: Analyze cluster logs for troubleshooting (registered only when ENABLE_TROUBLESHOOTING_TOOLS is set).

tools.name: cluster_events
tools.description: Get events for a cluster by ID.

tools.name: host_events
tools.description: Get events for a specific host in a cluster.

tools.name: cluster_iso_download_url
tools.description: Get discovery ISO download URL(s) for a cluster.

tools.name: cluster_credentials_download_url
tools.description: Get kubeconfig / credentials download URL after installation.

tools.name: cluster_logs_download_url
tools.description: Get a URL to download diagnostic log bundles.

tools.name: list_versions
tools.description: List OpenShift versions available for installation.

tools.name: list_operator_bundles
tools.description: List optional operator bundles addable at install time.

tools.name: add_operator_bundle_to_cluster
tools.description: Request an operator bundle to be installed with the cluster.

tools.name: set_host_role
tools.description: Set host role (auto-assign, master, arbiter, worker) for a discovered host.

tools.name: validate_nmstate_yaml
tools.description: Validate nmstate YAML before applying to hosts.

tools.name: generate_nmstate_yaml
tools.description: Generate initial nmstate YAML from gathered inputs (prefer calling this before hand-authoring YAML).

tools.name: alter_static_network_config_nmstate_for_host
tools.description: Update per-host static network nmstate configuration.

tools.name: list_static_network_config
tools.description: List static network configuration for the cluster or hosts.

openshift-administration.title: OpenShift MCP Server (administration)
openshift-administration.description: OpenShift MCP focused on multi-cluster administration and reporting: Kubernetes core plus config toolsets, read-only mode (--read-only), and kubeconfig-mounted access for fleet visibility. Used by cluster-report and similar skills.

tools.name: configuration_contexts_list
tools.description: List kubeconfig contexts and API server URLs for multi-cluster reporting

tools.name: configuration_view
tools.description: View merged kubeconfig and client configuration as YAML

tools.name: namespaces_list
tools.description: List all Kubernetes namespaces in the cluster

tools.name: projects_list
tools.description: List all OpenShift projects in the cluster

tools.name: events_list
tools.description: List Kubernetes events from all or specific namespaces

tools.name: resources_list
tools.description: List Kubernetes resources by apiVersion and kind

tools.name: resources_get
tools.description: Get specific Kubernetes resources by apiVersion, kind, name, and namespace

tools.name: resources_create_or_update
tools.description: Create or update resources from YAML or JSON (when not using read-only mode)

tools.name: resources_delete
tools.description: Delete Kubernetes resources (when not using read-only mode)

tools.name: pods_list
tools.description: List all pods in the cluster from all namespaces

tools.name: pods_get
tools.description: Get a specific pod by name and namespace

tools.name: pods_log
tools.description: Get logs from pod containers

tools.name: pods_exec
tools.description: Execute commands in pod containers

tools.name: pods_top
tools.description: List resource consumption for pods

tools.name: nodes_top
tools.description: List CPU and memory consumption for nodes

tools.name: nodes_log
tools.description: Get logs from Kubernetes nodes (kubelet, kube-proxy, system logs)

tools.name: nodes_stats_summary
tools.description: Get detailed resource usage statistics from nodes

podman.title: Podman MCP Server
podman.description: Local container engine MCP: build, pull, push, and remove images; run, inspect, log, stop, and remove containers; list networks and volumes—without requiring a Kubernetes cluster.

tools.name: container_inspect
tools.description: Displays the low-level information and configuration of a Docker or Podman container with the specified container ID or name

tools.name: container_list
tools.description: Prints out information about the running Docker or Podman containers

tools.name: container_logs
tools.description: Displays the logs of a Docker or Podman container with the specified container ID or name

tools.name: container_remove
tools.description: Removes a Docker or Podman container with the specified container ID or name

tools.name: container_run
tools.description: Runs a Docker or Podman container with the specified image name

tools.name: container_stop
tools.description: Stops a Docker or Podman running container with the specified container ID or name

tools.name: image_build
tools.description: Build a Docker or Podman image from a Dockerfile, Podmanfile, or Containerfile

tools.name: image_list
tools.description: List the Docker or Podman images on the local machine

tools.name: image_pull
tools.description: Copies (pulls) a Docker or Podman container image from a registry onto the local machine storage

tools.name: image_push
tools.description: Pushes a Docker or Podman container image from local machine storage to a registry

tools.name: image_remove
tools.description: Removes a Docker or Podman image from the local machine storage

tools.name: network_list
tools.description: List all the available Docker or Podman networks

tools.name: volume_list
tools.description: List all the available Docker or Podman volumes

github.title: GitHub MCP Server
github.description: GitHub API MCP for repositories, issues, pull requests, code search, branches, releases, and related collaboration workflows using a GitHub token.

tools.name: add_comment_to_pending_review
tools.description: Add review comment to the requester's latest pending pull request review

tools.name: add_issue_comment
tools.description: Add a comment to a specific issue in a GitHub repository

tools.name: assign_copilot_to_issue
tools.description: Assign Copilot to a specific issue in a GitHub repository

tools.name: create_branch
tools.description: Create a new branch in a GitHub repository

tools.name: create_or_update_file
tools.description: Create or update a single file in a GitHub repository

tools.name: create_pull_request
tools.description: Create a new pull request in a GitHub repository

tools.name: create_repository
tools.description: Create a new GitHub repository in your account or specified organization

tools.name: delete_file
tools.description: Delete a file from a GitHub repository

tools.name: fork_repository
tools.description: Fork a GitHub repository to your account or specified organization

tools.name: get_commit
tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

tools.name: get_file_contents
tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

tools.name: get_label
tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository

tools.name: get_latest_release
tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository

tools.name: get_me
tools.description: Get details of the authenticated GitHub user

tools.name: get_release_by_tag
tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository

tools.name: get_tag
tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository

tools.name: get_team_members
tools.description: Get member usernames of a specific team in an organization

tools.name: get_teams
tools.description: Get details of the teams the user is a member of

tools.name: issue_read
tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository

tools.name: issue_write
tools.description: Create a new or update an existing issue in a GitHub repository

tools.name: list_branches
tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

tools.name: list_commits
tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository

tools.name: list_issue_types
tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization)

tools.name: list_issues
tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository

tools.name: list_pull_requests
tools.description: List pull requests in a GitHub repository

tools.name: list_releases
tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

tools.name: list_tags
tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

tools.name: merge_pull_request
tools.description: Merge a pull request in a GitHub repository

tools.name: pull_request_read
tools.description: Get information on a specific pull request in GitHub repository

tools.name: pull_request_review_write
tools.description: Create and/or submit, delete review of a pull request

tools.name: push_files
tools.description: Push multiple files to a GitHub repository in a single commit

tools.name: request_copilot_review
tools.description: Request a GitHub Copilot code review for a pull request

tools.name: search_code
tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine

tools.name: search_issues
tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax

tools.name: search_pull_requests
tools.description: Search for pull requests in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax

tools.name: search_repositories
tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata

tools.name: search_users
tools.description: Find GitHub users by username, real name, or other profile information

tools.name: sub_issue_write
tools.description: Add a sub-issue to a parent issue in a GitHub repository

tools.name: update_pull_request
tools.description: Update an existing pull request in a GitHub repository

tools.name: update_pull_request_branch
tools.description: Update the branch of a pull request with the latest changes from the base branch

lightspeed-mcp.title: Red Hat Lightspeed MCP
lightspeed-mcp.description: Red Hat Insights and Lightspeed MCP: Advisor recommendations, vulnerability and compliance posture, inventory and drift, remediation guidance, subscription usage, and related platform insights for registered RHEL and hybrid cloud estates.

tools.name: advisor_get_active_rules
tools.description: Get active Advisor Recommendations for your account that help identify issues affecting system availability, stability, performance, or security. Use filters to find recommendations by impact level, likelihood, systems affected, workspace, tags, and automatic remediation availability. Higher impact/likelihood values indicate more critical issues.

tools.name: advisor_get_hosts_details_for_rule
tools.description: Get detailed information about RHEL systems affected by a specific Advisor Recommendation. Returns paginated system details with comprehensive information about each affected system, including system identification, impact metrics, RHEL version, and last seen timestamps. Each system entry contains hit counts categorized by severity level and incident status.

tools.name: advisor_get_hosts_hitting_a_rule
tools.description: Get all RHEL systems affected by a specific Advisor Recommendation. Shows which systems in your infrastructure have the issue identified by this recommendation. Use this to understand the scope of impact.

tools.name: advisor_get_recommendations_stats
tools.description: Show statistics of recommendations across categories and risks.

tools.name: advisor_get_rule_by_text_search
tools.description: Finds Advisor Recommendations that contain an exact text substring.

tools.name: advisor_get_rule_details
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific Advisor Recommendation, including impact level, likelihood, remediation steps, and related knowledge base articles.

tools.name: advisor_get_rule_from_node_id
tools.description: Find Advisor Recommendations related to a specific Knowledge Base article or solution. Use this when you have a Knowledge Base article or solution ID and want to find corresponding Advisor Recommendations that provide system-specific remediation steps.

tools.name: content-sources_list_repositories
tools.description: List repositories with filtering and pagination options.

tools.name: get_mcp_version
tools.description: Get the version of the Red Hat Lightspeed MCP server. Always call this if the user asks for the version of the Red Hat Lightspeed MCP server or when there is an API or authentication issue. Present the comparison URL to the user.

tools.name: image-builder_blueprint_compose
tools.description: Compose an image from a blueprint UUID created with create_blueprint, get_blueprints. If the UUID is not clear, ask the user whether to create a new blueprint with create_blueprint or use an existing blueprint from get_blueprints.

tools.name: image-builder_create_blueprint
tools.description: Create a custom Linux image blueprint. CRITICAL: Only call this function after you have gathered ALL required information from the user including blueprint name, distribution, architecture, image type, username, and any customizations. For RHEL images, use RHSM get_activation_keys and get_org_id to get required information. CUSTOM REPOSITORIES MUST BE INCLUDED IN BOTH payload_repositories AND custom_repositories fields.

tools.name: image-builder_get_blueprint_details
tools.description: Get blueprint details.

tools.name: image-builder_get_blueprints
tools.description: Show user's image blueprints (saved image templates/configurations for Linux distributions, packages, users).

tools.name: image-builder_get_compose_details
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific image build. REQUIRES: You MUST have the compose UUID from get_composes() first. NEVER call this with generic terms like "latest", "recent", or "my build". Returns detailed compose information including full status and progress, error messages if failed, download URLs if completed, build logs, and artifact details.

tools.name: image-builder_get_composes
tools.description: Get a list of all image builds (composes) with their UUIDs and basic status. ALWAYS USE THIS FIRST when checking image build status or finding builds. This returns the UUID needed for get_compose_details.

tools.name: image-builder_get_distributions
tools.description: Get the list of distributions available to build images with. Emphasize that there is support only for Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) images and there only for the latest minor version of each major version. Emphasize that Fedora images are "similar" to the upstream but no official versions! Emphasize that CentOS Stream is not supported by Red Hat.

tools.name: image-builder_get_openapi
tools.description: Get OpenAPI spec. Use this to get details e.g for a new blueprint. Optional parameters: endpoints - Comma-separated endpoint specs (like GET:/blueprints,POST:/blueprints). When provided, the returned OpenAPI is minimized to only the selected paths and their transitive component references. Use this only to prepare payloads for create_blueprint or update_blueprint.

tools.name: image-builder_get_org_id
tools.description: Get the organization ID for RHEL image registration/subscription. Purpose: Fetch the organization ID for RHEL image registration. When to Use: Always use this tool when enabling registration for Red Hat services in a blueprint. CRITICAL NOTE: Never assume or use placeholder organization IDs. Always fetch the actual organization ID using this tool.

tools.name: image-builder_update_blueprint
tools.description: Update a blueprint. VERIFY PARAMETERS - Get original blueprint details and UUID before proceeding. Use get_openapi(endpoints="PUT:/blueprints/{id}") to fetch the minimal schema needed to format the update payload.

tools.name: inventory_find_host_by_name
tools.description: Find a host by its hostname/display name.

tools.name: inventory_get_host_details
tools.description: Get detailed information for specific hosts by their IDs. Returns comprehensive host data including identifiers (insights_id, satellite_id, bios_uuid), display names, network info (IP/MAC addresses), cloud provider details, account/org metadata, timestamps (created, updated, stale_timestamp), reporter info, groups, facts, and basic system_profile data.

tools.name: inventory_get_host_system_profile
tools.description: Get detailed system profile information for specific hosts. Returns comprehensive hardware and software configuration data including CPU details (model, count, cores per socket), memory info (system_memory_bytes), infrastructure details (type, vendor), network interfaces, disk devices, BIOS information, and various system state data. For RHEL hosts, also includes software information such as enabled repositories, installed packages, and enabled services. This provides the most detailed technical specifications for each host.

tools.name: inventory_get_host_tags
tools.description: Get tags for specific hosts.

tools.name: inventory_list_hosts
tools.description: List hosts with filtering and sorting options. CRITICAL: For the 'per_page' parameter, you MUST use a value of 10 on the first call to avoid performance degradation and context overflow. Only use a larger value if the user explicitly requests to see more systems at once.

tools.name: planning_get_appstreams_lifecycle
tools.description: Get Application Streams lifecycle information. Use this tool when the user asks about Application Streams lifecycle (modules or packages) or wants to understand what streams exist for specific RHEL versions. Returns JSON-encoded response object containing metadata and a list of Application Stream lifecycle records with name, display_name, application_stream_name, stream, start_date, end_date, support_status, and lifecycle information.

tools.name: planning_get_relevant_upcoming_changes
tools.description: List relevant upcoming package changes, deprecations, additions and enhancements to user's systems. Use this tool to answer questions about upcoming package changes, deprecations, additions, or enhancements in the roadmap filtered by relevance to the user's systems. Also to plan for future upgrades and mitigate risk. Use this tool over get_upcoming_changes when the user asks about upcoming changes for their systems.

tools.name: planning_get_rhel_lifecycle
tools.description: Returns life cycle dates for all RHEL majors and minors. Use this tool when the user asks for RHEL versions and lifecycle timelines, including major versions, minor versions, or extended support types (EUS/E4S/ELS). Returns a response object containing a list of RHEL lifecycle records with name, start_date, end_date, support_status, display_name, major, minor, and extended support dates (end_date_e4s, end_date_els, end_date_eus).

tools.name: planning_get_upcoming_changes
tools.description: List upcoming package changes, deprecations, additions and enhancements. Use this tool to answer questions about upcoming package changes, deprecations, additions, or enhancements in the roadmap when a full list of upcoming items is acceptable. When the user asks about a specific RHEL version (for example, "What is coming in RHEL 9.4?"), call this tool without parameters and then filter and summarize the entries relevant to that version in your response.

tools.name: rbac_get_all_access
tools.description: Get access information for all Red Hat insights applications. This endpoint returns access information across all Red Hat insights applications. The API returns gzipped responses for this endpoint, which are handled by the client. Use this when you need to see access permissions across all applications.

tools.name: remediations_create_vuln_playbook
tools.description: Create remediation playbook for given CVEs on given systems to mitigate vulnerabilities. Don't process the playbook. You MUST return the YAML as is. Ask user if they want to get a link to the playbook or to get the YAML content. Inform them about the limitations of the link and that printing the YAML can be slow. If user ask for it, you can also respond with a link to the playbook. Inform user that they can't see the playbook with user other than Service Account used to create it.

tools.name: rhsm_get_activation_key
tools.description: Get a specific activation key by name. This endpoint returns details for a specific activation key including its name, description, service level, role, usage, release version, and additional repositories. Returns activation key details including configuration and subscription information.

tools.name: rhsm_get_activation_keys
tools.description: Get the list of activation keys available to the authenticated user. This endpoint returns activation keys that can be used for RHEL system registration. Activation keys contain subscription and configuration information needed to register systems with Red Hat Subscription Management. If the user has more questions about the activation keys, ask the user to go to https://console.redhat.com/insights/connector/activation-keys

tools.name: vulnerability_explain_cves
tools.description: Explain why CVEs are affecting my environment. This endpoint returns a detailed explanation of why CVEs are affecting my environment. It uses VMAAS to explain the CVEs, what packages are affected and why. Alongside with the information how this CVE can be fixed. To get the explanation, we need to get the system UUID from the inventory and list of CVEs. 'affected_packages' in 'vmaas' response is a list of packages that are affected by the CVE. To update affected packages, suggest to use Ansible Remediation Playbook via Remediations MCP tool.

tools.name: vulnerability_get_cve_systems
tools.description: Get list of systems affected by a given CVE. This is a report of affected systems for a given CVE. Use this tool to obtain list of all affected systems for a given CVE.

tools.name: vulnerability_get_cve
tools.description: Get details about specific CVE. This endpoint returns the CVE identification number, description, scores and other metadata. The metadata includes the description, CVSS 2/3 Score, CVSS 2/3 attack vector, severity, public date, modified date, business risk, status, a URL to Red Hat web pages, a list of advisories remediating the CVE, and information regarding known exploits for the CVE.

tools.name: vulnerability_get_cves
tools.description: Get list of CVEs affecting the account. This provides an overview of vulnerabilities across your entire system inventory. Use this endpoint to get an overview of which CVEs are affecting your account, including some CVE metadata, how many systems are affected by each CVE, and more.

tools.name: vulnerability_get_openapi
tools.description: Get Red Hat Lightspeed Vulnerability OpenAPI specification in JSON format.

tools.name: vulnerability_get_system_cves
tools.description: Get list of CVEs affecting a given system. IMPORTANT: Prefer get_cves as get_cves can filter for CVEs with available advisories. This is a report of CVEs affecting a given system. Use this tool to obtain list of all CVEs affecting a given system.

tools.name: vulnerability_get_systems
tools.description: Get list of systems in Red Hat Lightspeed Vulnerability inventory. List all systems registered in Red Hat Lightspeed Vulnerability service, including information about their last check-in, system name, workspace name, RHEL version, and number of CVEs affecting them. This tool shows both affected and not affected systems.

aap-mcp-job-management.title: AAP MCP Service-Job Management
aap-mcp-job-management.description: Ansible Automation Platform (AAP) job management MCP server for managing job templates, workflows, and job execution

tools.name: job_templates_list
tools.description: List job templates that define playbook execution configurations

tools.name: job_templates_retrieve
tools.description: Retrieve details of a specific job template by ID

tools.name: job_templates_launch_retrieve
tools.description: Launch a job from a job template

tools.name: jobs_list
tools.description: List job executions and their current status

tools.name: jobs_retrieve
tools.description: Retrieve the details of a job

tools.name: jobs_relaunch_retrieve
tools.description: Relaunch a job with the same parameters

tools.name: jobs_stdout_retrieve
tools.description: Retrieve the console output (stdout) from a job execution

tools.name: jobs_job_events_list
tools.description: List individual task events that occurred during a job execution

tools.name: jobs_job_host_summaries_list
tools.description: List per-host summary statistics for a job execution (ok, changed, failed counts)

tools.name: workflow_job_templates_list

openshift-virtualization.title: OpenShift MCP Server - Virtualization
openshift-virtualization.description: OpenShift MCP with core and KubeVirt toolsets for OpenShift Virtualization: standard cluster operations (namespaces, pods, resources, events) plus VirtualMachine create and lifecycle. Typical configuration uses --toolsets core,kubevirt.

tools.name: events_list
tools.description: List Kubernetes events from all or specific namespaces

tools.description: List all Kubernetes namespaces in the cluster

tools.name: projects_list
tools.description: List all OpenShift projects in the cluster

tools.description: List pods in a specific namespace

tools.description: Get a specific pod by name and namespace

tools.name: pods_log
tools.description: Get logs from pod containers

tools.name: resources_list
tools.description: List Kubernetes resources by apiVersion and kind

tools.name: resources_get
tools.description: Get specific Kubernetes resources

tools.name: vm_clone
tools.description: Clone a KubeVirt VirtualMachine via a VirtualMachineClone resource (source name/namespace, target name).

tools.name: vm_create
tools.description: Create new VirtualMachine resources in OpenShift Virtualization. Accepts namespace (required), name (required), workload/OS (default: fedora), size (small/medium/large), storage (default: 30Gi), autostart flag (default: false), optional networks (Multus NetworkAttachmentDefinitions), performance family hint (u1/o1/c1/m1), instance type, and preference. Automatically resolves instance types, preferences, and container disk images for common OS distributions (fedora, ubuntu, centos, centos-stream, debian, rhel, opensuse). Returns created VirtualMachine resource or error details.

tools.name: vm_lifecycle
tools.description: Manage VirtualMachine power state and lifecycle operations in OpenShift Virtualization. Accepts namespace (required), name (required), and action (required: start/stop/restart). Start sets runStrategy to Always (VM runs and auto-restarts on crash), stop sets runStrategy to Halted (VM stops and stays off), restart performs stop then start sequence. Returns updated VirtualMachine state or error if VM not found, permission denied, or already in desired state.
